indicators.title: Fertility rate, total (births per woman)
indicators.shortHook: Where families are still growing fast.

reviewNotes: Iconic demographic-transition map.
Close demographic distractors are acceptable only at hard tiers.

indicators.title: Adolescent fertility rate (births per 1,000 women ages 15-19)
indicators.shortHook: A youth-pattern map with a sharper social signal.

reviewNotes: Strong regional signal and a sharper story than general birth rates.
Avoid pairing with birth-rate or children-share outside Cartographer.

indicators.title: Birth rate, crude (per 1,000 people)
indicators.shortHook: The broadest signal of where populations are adding children.

reviewNotes: Too close to fertility rate and children-share for Daily.
Still useful as a Practice comparison map for demographic transition.

indicators.title: Death rate, crude (per 1,000 people)
indicators.shortHook: A deceptively hard age-structure clue.

reviewNotes: Crude death rate is easy to misread because age structure dominates.
Good lesson map, not a default Daily map yet.

indicators.title: Life expectancy at birth, total (years)
indicators.shortHook: A familiar health map with strong regional anchors.

reviewNotes: Classic health map with fair global anchors.
Avoid pairing with adult mortality as an easy-tier distractor.

indicators.title: Mortality rate, infant (per 1,000 live births)
indicators.shortHook: A child-health map with unmistakable low and high anchors.

reviewNotes: Almost identical to under-5 mortality.
Practice only so players can learn the distinction.

indicators.title: Mortality rate, under-5 (per 1,000 live births)
indicators.shortHook: A stark map of child survival.

reviewNotes: Strong child-survival map and clearer than infant mortality for Daily.
Infant mortality is acceptable only as a hard-tier close distractor.

indicators.title: Mortality rate, adult, male (per 1,000 male adults)
indicators.shortHook: A mortality map with conflict, health, and risk-behavior clues.

reviewNotes: Distinctive enough for expert play because male risk patterns matter.
Too correlated with other mortality maps for default Daily.

indicators.title: Mortality rate, adult, female (per 1,000 female adults)
indicators.shortHook: A parallel mortality map where gender differences matter.

reviewNotes: Very close to life expectancy and male adult mortality.
Playable only as an expert mortality puzzle.

indicators.title: Population ages 0-14 (% of total population)
indicators.shortHook: The young-population map.

reviewNotes: Too correlated with birth and fertility maps for default Daily.
Useful Practice anchor for age-structure learning.

indicators.title: Population ages 65 and above (% of total population)
indicators.shortHook: The aging map.

reviewNotes: Readable aging map and strong contrast to youth-heavy regions.
Do not pair with birth-rate as a low-tier distractor.

indicators.title: Age dependency ratio (% of working-age population)
indicators.shortHook: A map of who supports whom.

reviewNotes: Good synthesis of youth and aging pressure.
Fair when distractors avoid pure fertility lookalikes.

indicators.title: Rural population (% of total population)
indicators.shortHook: The inverse of the urban world.

reviewNotes: Exact inverse of urban population creates filler rather than a new puzzle.
Retired in favor of urban population.

indicators.title: Urban population (% of total population)
indicators.shortHook: The city-share map.

reviewNotes: Core settlement geography map.
Retire rural inverse to keep this version from feeling duplicated.

indicators.title: Population density (people per sq. km of land area)
indicators.shortHook: A map where small crowded places shout.

reviewNotes: Highly map-native and strategically probeable.
Small crowded countries make this satisfying.

indicators.title: School enrollment, primary (% gross)
indicators.shortHook: A schooling map with a surprisingly noisy edge.

reviewNotes: Gross enrollment can exceed intuitive school attendance readings.
Coverage is thinner and map pattern needs editorial review.

indicators.title: School enrollment, secondary (% gross)
indicators.shortHook: The stronger education access map.

reviewNotes: Stronger education signal than primary enrollment.
Coverage is just adequate, so keep missing-data caveat visible.

indicators.title: School enrollment, tertiary (% gross)
indicators.shortHook: A higher-education map with rich-country and city-state clues.

reviewNotes: Interesting higher-education map but uses an older selected year.
Expert-only until year freshness improves.

indicators.title: Government expenditure on education, total (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A budget-priority map, not a wealth map.

reviewNotes: Budget-share map can surprise players but does not equal quality.
Needs careful caveat in reveal.

indicators.title: Current health expenditure (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A health-system spending map.

reviewNotes: Good non-obvious health-system spending puzzle.
Reveal caveat prevents reading it as health quality.

indicators.title: Out-of-pocket expenditure (% of current health expenditure)
indicators.shortHook: A map of who pays at the clinic.

reviewNotes: Interesting health-finance map but not intuitive enough for default Daily.
Keep for expert players who enjoy system-design clues.

indicators.title: People using at least basic drinking water services (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: A basic-infrastructure map with clear missing edges.

reviewNotes: Clear basic-infrastructure story.
Avoid same-day pairing with electricity and sanitation when possible.

indicators.title: People using at least basic sanitation services (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: A public-health infrastructure map.

reviewNotes: Readable infrastructure map with clear development signal.
Do not pair closely with clean fuels, water access, or electricity on easier tiers.

indicators.title: Incidence of tuberculosis (per 100,000 people)
indicators.shortHook: A disease-burden map with distinctive regional pockets.

reviewNotes: Distinct disease-burden map that is not just GDP.
Good expert-style Daily with clear source/year.

indicators.title: Individuals using the Internet (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: The online-population map.

reviewNotes: Readable connectivity map with strong regional anchors.
High development correlation means income-style distractors need restraint.

indicators.title: Mobile cellular subscriptions (per 100 people)
indicators.shortHook: A connectivity map where values can exceed 100.

reviewNotes: Interesting because values can exceed 100 subscriptions per 100 people.
Distinct from internet users and fixed broadband.

indicators.title: Fixed broadband subscriptions (per 100 people)
indicators.shortHook: A wired-connectivity map.

reviewNotes: Useful contrast with mobile-first connectivity.
Coverage and infrastructure nuance make it expert-only.

indicators.title: Secure Internet servers (per 1 million people)
indicators.shortHook: A digital-infrastructure outlier map.

reviewNotes: Good outlier map for hosting and finance hubs.
Too technical for default Daily.

indicators.title: Access to electricity (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: A foundational infrastructure map.

reviewNotes: Clear infrastructure map with strong geographic anchors.
Avoid same-day pairing with water access and sanitation.

indicators.title: Access to clean fuels and technologies for cooking (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: A household-energy map.

reviewNotes: A household-energy map with a real public-health story.
High income correlation requires careful distractors.

indicators.title: Renewable energy consumption (% of total final energy consumption)
indicators.shortHook: A map where low-income biomass and modern renewables can both matter.

reviewNotes: Traditional biomass caveat makes the map counterintuitive.
Expert-only until the reveal copy can carry the nuance.

indicators.title: Renewable electricity output (% of total electricity output)
indicators.shortHook: The hydro-and-grid mix map.

reviewNotes: Good hydro/grid-mix geography puzzle.
Keep distinct from total renewable energy consumption.

indicators.title: Carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions excluding LULUCF per capita (t CO2e/capita)
indicators.shortHook: The per-person carbon map.

reviewNotes: High-interest climate map with resource-economy outliers.
GHG per capita is acceptable only as a hard-tier close distractor.

indicators.title: Total greenhouse gas emissions excluding LULUCF per capita (t CO2e/capita)
indicators.shortHook: A broader climate footprint map.

reviewNotes: Interesting broader climate footprint map.
Too close to CO2 per capita for Daily without a very deliberate hard-tier setup.

indicators.title: PM2.5 air pollution, mean annual exposure (micrograms per cubic meter)
indicators.shortHook: A dirty-air exposure map.

reviewNotes: Strong air-pollution geography with recognizable regional belts.
One of the cleanest Daily environmental maps.

indicators.title: Forest area (% of land area)
indicators.shortHook: The forest-cover map.

reviewNotes: Excellent map-led geography puzzle.
Strong contrast among humid tropics, boreal regions, islands, and arid states.

indicators.title: Arable land (% of land area)
indicators.shortHook: The crop-ready land map.

reviewNotes: Good physical-geography and farming-belt reasoning.
Works best with probes in deserts, dense deltas, and temperate crop regions.

indicators.title: Agricultural land (% of land area)
indicators.shortHook: A wider farming-and-pasture map.

reviewNotes: Useful land-use practice map.
Less Daily-worthy than arable land or forest area because pasture broadens the definition.

indicators.title: Cereal yield (kg per hectare)
indicators.shortHook: A farm-productivity map.

reviewNotes: Distinctive agricultural productivity pattern.
Excellent expert-style Daily when reveal explains crop mix caveat.

indicators.title: Agriculture, forestry, and fishing, value added (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: An economy-structure map.

reviewNotes: Strong economy-structure map that is not a raw-total GDP quiz.
Avoid unfair pairing with agricultural employment.

indicators.title: GDP per capita (current US$)
indicators.shortHook: The familiar income-per-person map.

reviewNotes: Familiar baseline map and useful anchor for other reveals.
Do not pair with PPP outside hard tiers.

indicators.title: GDP per capita, PPP (current international $)
indicators.shortHook: Income per person adjusted for local prices.

reviewNotes: Too close to nominal GDP per capita for default Daily.
Good Practice comparison for purchasing-power nuance.

indicators.title: Inflation, consumer prices (annual %)
indicators.shortHook: A volatile economy map.

reviewNotes: Volatile macro stress map can be fun but one-year shocks dominate.
Expert-only until editorial notes are more country-specific.

indicators.title: Unemployment, total (% of total labor force) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A labor-market map that is not just poverty.

reviewNotes: Readable labor-market map with non-obvious regional pockets.
Definition caveat helps distinguish it from poverty or non-work.

indicators.title: Labor force participation rate, total (% of total population ages 15+) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A map of who is counted in the labor force.

reviewNotes: Useful labor concept but less visually memorable than unemployment or agricultural employment.
Practice-only for now.

indicators.title: Labor force participation rate, female (% of female population ages 15+) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A gendered work map.

reviewNotes: Valuable gendered labor map.
Needs expert context because social norms and measurement vary.

indicators.title: Employment in agriculture (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: The farming-workforce map.

reviewNotes: Strong work-structure map that rewards reasoning about sector mix.
Do not pair with employment-services on easier tiers.

indicators.title: Employment in services (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: The service-economy workforce map.

reviewNotes: Nearly inverse of agricultural employment.
Useful in Practice, not Daily.

indicators.title: Vulnerable employment, total (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A labor-security map.

reviewNotes: Near-identical to self-employed workers and hard to explain quickly.
Retired until the labor catalog is hand-curated.

indicators.title: Self-employed, total (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A work-structure map.

reviewNotes: Near-identical to vulnerable employment.
Needs editorial decision before returning to playable catalog.

indicators.title: Trade (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: The open-economy map.

reviewNotes: Good open-economy map with small-state and port-economy clues.
Exports/imports only acceptable as hard-tier close distractors.

indicators.title: Exports of goods and services (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A map of economies selling outward.

reviewNotes: Close to trade share and imports share.
Playable only as a hard open-economy distinction.

indicators.title: Imports of goods and services (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A map of economies buying from abroad.

reviewNotes: Close to trade and exports share.
Keep as Expert Practice until open-economy distractors are hand-tuned.

indicators.title: Foreign direct investment, net inflows (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A volatile investment-flow map.

reviewNotes: Volatile but interesting for finance/resource-flow players.
Too one-year-spiky for default Daily.

indicators.title: Current account balance (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A surplus-and-deficit map.

reviewNotes: Interesting for macro nerds but too abstract for default Daily.
Keep in Expert Practice only.

indicators.title: Tax revenue (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A fiscal-capacity map.

reviewNotes: Coverage is exactly at the floor and reference year is older than most catalog items.
Hold until fiscal-capacity copy and data caveat are reviewed.

indicators.title: Population growth (annual %)
indicators.shortHook: The momentum map after births, deaths, and migration all combine.

reviewNotes: Readable demographic momentum map that differs from fertility because migration and deaths also matter.
Good Daily candidate if same-day selection avoids fertility and birth-rate lookalikes.

indicators.title: Maternal mortality ratio (modeled estimate, per 100,000 live births)
indicators.shortHook: A stark map of childbirth risk.

reviewNotes: Strong, understandable health-system map with clear high and low anchors.
Works for Daily when not paired with multiple child-mortality indicators.

indicators.title: Prevalence of undernourishment (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: A food-security map where missing and extreme values matter.

reviewNotes: Food-security pattern is legible and meaningfully different from income alone.
Keep the modeled/capped-value caveat visible in reveal copy.

indicators.title: Immunization, measles (% of children ages 12-23 months)
indicators.shortHook: A vaccine-coverage map with conflict and health-capacity clues.

reviewNotes: Routine vaccine coverage is concrete and readable.
DPT can be a close Cartographer distractor but should not share an easy Daily with this map.

indicators.title: Immunization, DPT (% of children ages 12-23 months)
indicators.shortHook: A routine childhood-vaccine map.

reviewNotes: Useful health-system practice map, but very close to measles immunization.
Hold out of Daily to avoid two vaccine maps feeling like duplicate content.

indicators.title: Physicians (per 1,000 people)
indicators.shortHook: The doctor-density map.

reviewNotes: Doctor density gives a direct health-workforce signal.
Good Daily candidate if coverage is broad enough and not paired with hospital beds.

indicators.title: Hospital beds (per 1,000 people)
indicators.shortHook: A health-capacity map with post-Soviet and rich-country clues.

reviewNotes: Distinctive expert map with health-system legacy patterns.
Too easy to overread as health quality, so keep it out of default Daily.

indicators.title: Industry (including construction), value added (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: The industry-structure map.

reviewNotes: Economy-structure map with good resource and manufacturing clues.
Close sector-share distractors are fair only on harder tiers.

indicators.title: Services, value added (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: The service-economy map.

reviewNotes: Useful Practice map, but often acts like the inverse of agriculture/industry structure.
Hold out of Daily until sector-share variety is manually tuned.

indicators.title: Manufacturing, value added (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A manufacturing-specific economy map.

reviewNotes: More specific and often more interesting than total industry share.
Daily-worthy as long as distractors avoid turning it into generic GDP structure.

indicators.title: GDP growth (annual %)
indicators.shortHook: A one-year macro momentum map.

reviewNotes: Volatile one-year macro map that can be interesting for experts.
Not Daily material because rebound years and shocks can feel arbitrary.

indicators.title: GNI per capita, Atlas method (current US$)
indicators.shortHook: Income per person through national income.

reviewNotes: Useful income comparison map but close to existing GDP per capita.
Practice-only to avoid padding Daily with near-identical income maps.

indicators.title: GNI per capita, PPP (current international $)
indicators.shortHook: National income adjusted for local prices.

reviewNotes: Good Practice comparison to GDP PPP.
Too similar to existing income maps for default Daily.

indicators.title: Personal remittances, received (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A migration-money map where small economies stand out.

reviewNotes: Interesting migration-money map with distinctive small-economy anchors.
Expert-only because the denominator and diaspora story require explanation.

indicators.title: Military expenditure (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A security-priority map, not a raw military-size map.

reviewNotes: High-interest but politically and contextually tricky map.
Expert-only because GDP-share spending is not raw military power.

indicators.title: High-technology exports (% of manufactured exports)
indicators.shortHook: A manufacturing-composition map with export-hub outliers.

reviewNotes: Interesting export-composition puzzle with non-obvious winners.
Expert-only because it depends on manufactured-export denominator, not total exports.

indicators.title: Proportion of seats held by women in national parliaments (%)
indicators.shortHook: A political-representation map with non-obvious leaders.

reviewNotes: Adds a governance/society map that broadens the catalog beyond economics and health.
Good Daily candidate because the geography is not a simple income gradient.

indicators.title: International migrant stock (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: The migrant-share map where Gulf states and small countries pop.

reviewNotes: Distinctive map with Gulf, small-state, and migration-hub anchors.
Daily-worthy because it teaches a different demographic pattern than fertility or aging.

indicators.title: Net migration
indicators.shortHook: A volatile migration-balance map.

reviewNotes: Positive and negative raw values are hard to communicate with the current one-direction choropleth.
Needs a diverging-map treatment or special copy before gameplay exposure.

indicators.title: International tourism, number of arrivals
indicators.shortHook: A travel-flow map where small and famous destinations stand out.

reviewNotes: Accessible and fun map with strong destination and transport clues.
Daily-worthy as long as reveal explains raw arrivals rather than per-capita tourism.

indicators.title: International tourism, receipts (% of total exports)
indicators.shortHook: A tourism-dependence map, not a visitor-count map.

reviewNotes: Good specialist tourism-dependence map.
Expert-only because percent of exports is less intuitive than arrival counts.

indicators.title: Electric power consumption (kWh per capita)
indicators.shortHook: The electricity-consumption map.

reviewNotes: Useful consumption-intensity map if the data is recent enough.
Pipeline freshness will decide whether this can actually remain Daily-eligible.

indicators.title: Energy use (kg of oil equivalent per capita)
indicators.shortHook: The total-energy-consumption map.

reviewNotes: Broad per-person energy demand map with good resource and climate clues.
Daily-worthy only if selected year and coverage survive validation.

indicators.title: Fossil fuel energy consumption (% of total)
indicators.shortHook: The fossil-dependence map.

reviewNotes: Good expert contrast against renewable electricity and total energy use.
Too technical for default Daily because it is total energy, not electricity mix.

indicators.title: Terrestrial protected areas (% of total land area)
indicators.shortHook: A conservation-designation map.

reviewNotes: Conservation-designation map broadens the environmental catalog.
Readable enough for Daily if the generated map has enough variation.

indicators.title: Marine protected areas (% of territorial waters)
indicators.shortHook: A marine-conservation map with island and coastal surprises.

reviewNotes: Interesting marine map, especially for island and coastal countries.
Practice-only because landlocked countries and zero-heavy values may need careful QA.

indicators.title: Renewable internal freshwater resources per capita (cubic meters)
indicators.shortHook: A water-abundance map shaped by rivers, rainfall, and population.

reviewNotes: Good physical-geography plus population-density reasoning.
Daily-worthy when paired away from generic water-access maps.

indicators.title: Annual freshwater withdrawals, total (% of internal resources)
indicators.shortHook: A water-pressure map where arid countries can dominate.

reviewNotes: Strong water-pressure idea, but values over 100 percent can surprise players.
Expert-only until reveal copy and legend behavior are reviewed.

indicators.title: Population living in areas where elevation is below 5 meters (% of total population)
indicators.shortHook: The coastal-exposure map.

reviewNotes: Compelling coastal-exposure map with a distinct topic.
Expert-only because players need to reason about percent of people near sea level, not coastline length.

indicators.title: Fertilizer consumption (kilograms per hectare of arable land)
indicators.shortHook: A tricky agriculture-input map with a unit trap.

reviewNotes: The requested code is a percent-of-production measure, not fertilizer applied per hectare.
Needs manual decision before exposing a likely unit trap to players.

indicators.title: Food production index (2014-2016 = 100)
indicators.shortHook: A food-output trend map.

reviewNotes: Useful trend-style agriculture map that is different from land share.
Daily-worthy if reveal copy makes the index base clear.

indicators.title: Crop production index (2014-2016 = 100)
indicators.shortHook: A crop-output trend map close to the food index.

reviewNotes: Too close to food production index for this expansion pass.
Retired to avoid a padded-feeling agriculture catalog.

indicators.title: Current health expenditure per capita (current US$)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for health spending per person.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Health spending per person adds useful practice depth with an understandable unit.
Practice-only until distractor ambiguity and new-player comprehension are checked in playtests.

indicators.title: Domestic general government health expenditure (% of current health expenditure)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for government health spending share.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Government health spending share is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.
Keep to advanced tiers and review confusion notes before broader promotion.

indicators.title: Domestic private health expenditure (% of current health expenditure)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for private health spending share.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Private health spending share is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.
Keep to advanced tiers and review confusion notes before broader promotion.

indicators.title: People using safely managed sanitation services (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for safely managed sanitation.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Safely managed sanitation adds useful practice depth with an understandable unit.
Practice-only until distractor ambiguity and new-player comprehension are checked in playtests.

indicators.title: People using safely managed drinking water services (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for safely managed drinking water.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Safely managed drinking water adds useful practice depth with an understandable unit.
Practice-only until distractor ambiguity and new-player comprehension are checked in playtests.

indicators.title: People practicing open defecation (% of population)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for open defecation.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Open defecation passed the source gate with broad enough coverage and a readable map pattern.
Daily-eligible, but keep close same-topic distractors out of easier tiers during outside beta.

indicators.title: Nurses and midwives (per 1,000 people)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for nurses and midwives.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: useful health-workforce idea, but the selected year is older than the rest of the beta catalog.
Needs review for freshness and reveal wording before gameplay.

indicators.title: Mortality rate, neonatal (per 1,000 live births)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for neonatal mortality.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: strong health metric, but it is extremely close to infant mortality and under-five mortality.
Expert-only if used at all; keep it out of ordinary Practice until mortality clusters are tuned.

indicators.title: Cause of death, by communicable diseases and maternal, prenatal and nutrition conditions (% of total)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for communicable disease deaths.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Communicable disease deaths is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.
Keep to advanced tiers and review confusion notes before broader promotion.

indicators.title: Cause of death, by non-communicable diseases (% of total)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for noncommunicable disease deaths.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: near-perfect inverse of communicable disease deaths and not distinct enough for the beta catalog.
Retired; keep the communicable-share map as the cleaner expert version.

indicators.title: Immunization, HepB3 (% of one-year-old children)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for hepatitis b immunization.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: too close to DPT and measles immunization to justify another vaccine map in beta.
Retired as redundant catalog padding.

indicators.title: School enrollment, primary (% net)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for net primary enrollment.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: School enrollment, preprimary (% gross)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for preprimary enrollment.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: School enrollment, primary, private (% of total primary)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for private primary enrollment.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: School enrollment, primary (gross), gender parity index (GPI)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for primary enrollment gender parity.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: School enrollment, tertiary (gross), gender parity index (GPI)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for tertiary enrollment gender parity.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: Permanent cropland (% of land area)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for permanent cropland.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Permanent cropland passed the source gate with broad enough coverage and a readable map pattern.
Daily-eligible, but keep close same-topic distractors out of easier tiers during outside beta.

indicators.title: Arable land (hectares per person)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for arable land per person.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Arable land per person passed the source gate with broad enough coverage and a readable map pattern.
Daily-eligible, but keep close same-topic distractors out of easier tiers during outside beta.

indicators.title: Livestock production index (2014-2016 = 100)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for livestock production index.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: map has coverage, but the production-index unit is less intuitive than land, yield, or employment measures.
Needs review before exposing it to beta players.

indicators.title: Agriculture, forestry, and fishing, value added per worker (constant 2015 US$)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for agriculture value per worker.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Agriculture value per worker is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.
Keep to advanced tiers and review confusion notes before broader promotion.

indicators.title: Agriculture, forestry, and fishing, value added (annual % growth)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for agriculture value growth.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: volatile annual growth map can feel arbitrary and shock-driven.
Needs review before returning to gameplay.

indicators.title: Adjusted savings: net forest depletion (% of GNI)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for net forest depletion.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: Forest rents (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for forest rents.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: Agricultural raw materials exports (% of merchandise exports)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for agricultural raw material exports.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Agricultural raw material exports is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.
Keep to advanced tiers and review confusion notes before broader promotion.

indicators.title: Agricultural raw materials imports (% of merchandise imports)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for agricultural raw material imports.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: too niche and less intuitive than agricultural raw material exports for beta play.
Retired; keep the export-side map as the expert comparison.

indicators.title: Average precipitation in depth (mm per year)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for average precipitation.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Average precipitation passed the source gate with broad enough coverage and a readable map pattern.
Daily-eligible, but keep close same-topic distractors out of easier tiers during outside beta.

indicators.title: Level of water stress: freshwater withdrawal as a proportion of available freshwater resources
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for water stress.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: compelling water-pressure pattern, but values can exceed 100 percent and it is a close lookalike for freshwater withdrawal maps.
Expert-only until beta testers confirm the unit and reveal feel fair.

indicators.title: Annual freshwater withdrawals, agriculture (% of total freshwater withdrawal)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for agricultural water withdrawals.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: understandable water-use pattern, but it forms a confusing trio with industrial and domestic withdrawal shares.
Practice-only until the withdrawal-share family is tested together.

indicators.title: Annual freshwater withdrawals, industry (% of total freshwater withdrawal)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for industrial water withdrawals.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Industrial water withdrawals adds useful practice depth with an understandable unit.
Practice-only until distractor ambiguity and new-player comprehension are checked in playtests.

indicators.title: Annual freshwater withdrawals, domestic (% of total freshwater withdrawal)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for domestic water withdrawals.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Domestic water withdrawals adds useful practice depth with an understandable unit.
Practice-only until distractor ambiguity and new-player comprehension are checked in playtests.

indicators.title: Water productivity, total (constant 2015 US$ GDP per cubic meter of total freshwater withdrawal)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for water productivity.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: useful expert comparison map, but dollars per cubic meter is not intuitive enough for ordinary Practice.
Expert-only until unit comprehension is tested.

indicators.title: Terrestrial and marine protected areas (% of total territorial area)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for protected land and seas.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Protected land and seas passed the source gate with broad enough coverage and a readable map pattern.
Daily-eligible, but keep close same-topic distractors out of easier tiers during outside beta.

indicators.title: PM2.5 air pollution, population exposed to levels exceeding WHO guideline value (% of total)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for population above pm2.5 guideline.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: Carbon intensity of GDP (kg CO2e per constant 2015 US$ of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for carbon intensity of gdp.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: strong climate map, but the GDP-intensity unit is too technical for a shared Daily without tester confirmation.
Keep as Expert-only until answer-choice wording and reveal copy prove fair for general players.

indicators.title: Total natural resources rents (% of GDP)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for natural resource rents.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: distinctive resource-economy map, but the unit is abstract and strongly overlaps with resource-depletion concepts.
Practice-only for beta; not broad enough for the shared Daily yet.

indicators.title: Adjusted savings: natural resources depletion (% of GNI)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for natural resource depletion.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: near-duplicate of clean fuels access with an even narrower rural slice.
Retired for beta so the catalog does not feel padded with obvious variants.

indicators.title: GDP per capita growth (annual %)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for gdp per person growth.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: too close to GDP growth while adding little mystery-map value.
Retired as redundant for beta.

indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for fixed capital formation.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Fixed capital formation is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.
Keep to advanced tiers and review confusion notes before broader promotion.

indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for gross savings.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Gross savings is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Domestic savings is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.

indicators.title: Account ownership at a financial institution or with a mobile-money-service provider (% of population ages 15+)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for account ownership.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Account ownership passed the source gate with broad enough coverage and a readable map pattern.
Daily-eligible, but keep close same-topic distractors out of easier tiers during outside beta.

indicators.title: Labor force participation rate, male (% of male population ages 15+) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for male labor force participation.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: Ratio of female to male labor force participation rate (%) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for female-to-male labor force ratio.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: useful gendered labor map, but it closely shadows female labor-force participation for many players.
Practice-only until outside testers show they can distinguish the ratio from the simpler female labor-force map.

indicators.title: Labor force participation rate for ages 15-24, total (%) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for youth labor force participation.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: useful but narrow youth labor map with strong overlap against labor-force participation.
Expert-only for beta.

indicators.title: Unemployment, youth total (% of total labor force ages 15-24) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for youth unemployment.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: readable labor map, but too easy to confuse with total unemployment in a default Daily.
Practice-only; keep it out of Daily until distractors and reveal framing are tuned.

indicators.title: Unemployment, male (% of male labor force) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for male unemployment.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: Unemployment, female (% of female labor force) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for female unemployment.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Female unemployment is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.
Keep to advanced tiers and review confusion notes before broader promotion.

indicators.title: Employment to population ratio, 15+, total (%) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for employment-to-population ratio.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: source-valid, but it closely shadows labor-force participation and wage-worker maps.
Expert-only until the labor cluster gets stricter answer-choice curation.

indicators.title: Wage and salaried workers, total (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for wage and salaried workers.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: near-inverse of self-employed workers and easy to overread as job quality.
Expert-only; keep out of general Practice until labor distractors are tuned.

indicators.title: Employers, total (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for employers share.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: source-valid and patterned, but the employer-share concept is narrow and hard to infer from geography alone.
Expert-only for beta; not broad enough for Daily.

indicators.title: Employment in industry (% of total employment) (modeled ILO estimate)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for employment in industry.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 Needs-review triage: broad coverage, fresh data, and a readable sector-structure pattern make this an obvious Practice candidate.
Keep out of Daily until it is tested against agriculture/services employment lookalikes.

indicators.title: Fixed telephone subscriptions (per 100 people)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for fixed telephone subscriptions.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Fixed telephone subscriptions adds useful practice depth with an understandable unit.
Practice-only until distractor ambiguity and new-player comprehension are checked in playtests.

indicators.title: Commercial bank branches (per 100,000 adults)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for commercial bank branches.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Commercial bank branches is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.
Keep to advanced tiers and review confusion notes before broader promotion.

indicators.title: Logistics performance index: Overall (1=low to 5=high)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for logistics performance.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: Logistics performance index: Quality of trade and transport-related infrastructure (1=low to 5=high)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for logistics infrastructure quality.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: low unit clarity and high overlap with the broader logistics index make this feel arbitrary.
Needs review; do not expose until logistics maps have a clearer gameplay frame.

indicators.title: Population in the largest city (% of urban population)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for largest city share.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Largest city share adds useful practice depth with an understandable unit.
Practice-only until distractor ambiguity and new-player comprehension are checked in playtests.

indicators.title: Population living in slums (% of urban population)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for urban slum population.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 Needs-review triage: strong and socially meaningful settlement map with adequate coverage.
Practice-only for beta because the topic needs careful reveal framing and tester feedback before Daily use.

indicators.title: Urban population growth (annual %)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for urban population growth.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: readable map, but it is too close to total population growth for a default Daily mystery.
Practice-only until same-day and answer-choice safeguards prove it will not feel arbitrary.

indicators.title: International tourism, expenditures (% of total imports)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for tourism expenditures.

reviewNotes: Loaded from the candidate intake queue and default-held as Needs review until a human editor classifies it.

indicators.title: Land area where elevation is below 5 meters (% of total land area)
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for low-elevation land.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 promotion: Low-elevation land is source-valid but more technical or correlated than a default Daily map.
Keep to advanced tiers and review confusion notes before broader promotion.

indicators.title: WBL: Legal Framework Index (scale 0-100)
indicators.shortTitle: Women, Business and the Law index
indicators.shortHook: A candidate map for women, business and the law index.

reviewNotes: Batch 2 QA: high-interest topic, but the composite index definition is long and source-specific.
Expert-only until the reveal can make the legal-framework index feel fair as a mystery map.

indicators.title: Statistical performance indicators (SPI): Pillar 5 data infrastructure score (scale 0-100)
